architecture.summary: An interactive perception system where a large language model serves as the backbone to plan and reason over multimodal robot sensing (vision, sound, haptics, proprioception). The LLM instructs epistemic actions to sample sensory information in a partially observable environment, reasons over the resulting multimodal observations, and plans task execution. Multimodal modules ground the LLM's processing by providing sensory context that extends its ability to decide on information-gathering actions before task execution.

biblio.title: Chat with the Environment: Interactive Multimodal Perception Using Large Language Models

claims.statement: LLMs can provide high-level planning and reasoning skills and control interactive robot behavior in a multimodal environment, while multimodal modules with the context of the environmental state help ground the LLMs and extend their processing ability.

An interactive perception framework enables a robot to decide on a range of epistemic actions in order to sample sensory information among multiple modalities before being able to execute the task correctly in a partially observable state.
